name: Kingdom of Italy (Napoleonic, 1805–1814)
description: A green, white and red vertical tricolour derived from the earlier 'carre' arrangement — the red field with a white lozenge enclosing a green square — charged at the centre with a golden Napoleonic eagle.
history: In 1805 the Italian Republic was transformed into the Kingdom of Italy with Napoleon I as king. The state flag retained the colours of the Republic in rectangular form and added the golden imperial (Napoleonic) eagle, while the plain green-white-red tricolour continued in civil use. The kingdom was dissolved in 1814 following Napoleon's abdication, and its territory was largely annexed to the Austrian Empire.

features.symbolism: the red field with a white lozenge enclosing a green square, carrying the Italian tricolour colours in the 'carre' arrangement inherited from the Italian Republic
Napoleonic imperial sovereignty over the Kingdom of Italy
